title: /etc/containers/policy.json.  not found
body:
![image](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/assets/38821215/7402dd62-1db6-47f3-9d4c-aa33ce402e8e)



I'm not sure if this is a new bug, but I do have this problem with the latest version of the code
 
- https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/394
- https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/1315

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Nothing in the above indicates how that version of Skopeo was installed. And yes, that file needs to be installed for Skopeo to work.

```
sudo rm -rf /usr/local/go*
sudo rm -rf ./go* 

export version=1.20
mkdir /usr/local/go$version


wget https://golang.google.cn/dl/go${version}.linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/go${version}

sudo tar -xvf ./go${version}.linux-amd64.tar.gz -C /usr/local/go${version} --strip-components 1
 
 
sudo mkdir -p ~/.go/{bin,src,pkg}

sudo cat <<EOF >> ~/.bashrc

export GOROOT="/usr/local/go${version}"
export GOPATH=\$HOME/.go   
export GOBIN=\$GOROOT/bin
export PATH=\$PATH:\$GOBIN
EOF

source ~/.bashrc

go version
go env
go env -w GO111MODULE=on
go env -w GOPROXY=https://goproxy.cn,direct
go env -w GOFLAGS="-buildvcs=false"

sudo rm -rf ./go* 


sudo apt install libgpgme-dev libassuan-dev libbtrfs-dev libdevmapper-dev pkg-config -y
rm -rf main.zip skopeo* v*
wget https://github.com/containers/skopeo/archive/refs/tags/v1.11.2.zip

unzip v1.11.2.zip

cd skopeo-1.11.2/cmd/skopeo
go build .
sudo mv skopeo /usr/bin/
cd -

rm -rf main.zip skopeo* v*

sudo skopeo login -u  *ignore* registry.cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com
 
sudo skopeo sync --src docker --dest docker kindest/node registry.cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/acejilam --src-tls-verify=false --dest-tls-verify=false 
sudo skopeo sync --src docker --dest docker registry.k8s.io/metrics-server registry.cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/acejilam --src-tls-verify=false --dest-tls-verify=false 
```

https://shell.cloud.google.com/?show=ide%2Cterminal

The official installation instructions are https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/install.md . That says `make all` and `make install`.

If you are doing something different, then the effects of that (good or bad) are up to you.

```cat > sync.sh << EOF
sed -i 's/mirrorlist/#mirrorlist/g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-*
sed -i 's|#baseurl=http://mirror.centos.org|baseurl=http://vault.centos.org|g' /etc/yum.repos.d/CentOS-*
yum makecache
yum update -y
yum -y install vim wget unzip skopeo -y 
 
skopeo login -u xxxxxx registry.cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com
 
skopeo sync --src docker --dest docker kindest/node registry.cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/acejilam --src-tls-verify=false --dest-tls-verify=false 
skopeo sync --src docker --dest docker registry.k8s.io/metrics-server registry.cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/acejilam --src-tls-verify=false --dest-tls-verify=false 

EOF

 
docker run  --name centos -v `pwd`:/root -it -d centos:8 sh 

docker exec -it centos bash /root/sync.sh
```
